name: Dr. Mario
description: Dr. Mario is a puzzle game where the player, controlling Dr. Mario, attempts to eradicate viruses from a bottle-shaped playing field. Viruses are stationary and come in three colors: red, yellow, and blue. The player dispenses dual-colored capsules, which are combinations of two capsule halves, each half being one of the three virus colors. The capsules fall from the top of the playing field. The player rotates and positions these capsules as they fall to create lines of four or more matching colors (viruses or capsule halves) either vertically or horizontally. When a line of four or more matching colors is formed, those pieces are removed from the playing field. The player continues dispensing capsules until all viruses are eliminated, resulting in a win. If the capsules fill the playing field to the point where the next capsule cannot enter, the game is over, and the player loses. The game features varying starting virus counts and speeds at which the capsules fall. The number of starting viruses corresponds to difficulty.

controls.arrow_left: Move capsule one position to the left
controls.arrow_right: Move capsule one position to the right
controls.space: Immediately drop the capsule to the lowest available position in its current column
